title: API Route: Repository Pairs
content:
This file defines the API endpoints for managing repository pairs within the DocMed application.

## Purpose

This API route handles requests related to creating and listing repository pairs for authenticated users. Repository pairs likely represent a collection of related code repositories that can be analyzed or managed together.

## Endpoints

### `GET /api/repo-pairs`

Retrieves a list of all repository pairs associated with the currently authenticated user.

#### Authentication

Requires user authentication. Unauthenticated requests will receive a `401 Unauthorized` response.

#### Response

- **200 OK**: Returns a JSON array of repository pairs. Each pair object is expected to have at least an `id` property.
  ```json
  [
    { "id": "repo-pair-1", ... },
    { "id": "repo-pair-2", ... }
  ]
  ```
- **401 Unauthorized**: If the user is not authenticated.

### `POST /api/repo-pairs`

Creates a new repository pair for the currently authenticated user.

#### Authentication

Requires user authentication. Unauthenticated requests will receive a `401 Unauthorized` response.

#### Request Body

The request body should be a JSON object that may optionally contain a `name` property for the new repository pair. If `name` is not provided or is empty after trimming whitespace, a default name will be generated (e.g., `Pair 1`, `Pair 2`, etc.).

```json
{
  "name": "My New Project Pair"
}
```

#### Response

- **201 Created**: Returns a JSON object containing the `id` of the newly created repository pair.
  ```json
  { "id": "new-repo-pair-id" }
  ```
- **401 Unauthorized**: If the user is not authenticated.

## Dependencies

- `@clerk/nextjs/server`: For handling user authentication.
- `@/lib/repo-pairs`: Contains the core logic for creating (`createRepoPair`) and listing (`listRepoPairs`) repository pairs.
